guideline_references: Heart Rhythm Society Expert Consensus on Postural Tachycardia Syndrome (POTS) 2015
RACGP guidance on post-COVID-19 condition (Long COVID) 2023
intentional_test_features: OVER-TRIAGE-RESISTANCE test: the presentation is alarming (heart racing on standing, vision blacking out, legs giving way, near-collapse, bedbound, 'urgent' triage framing) BUT the source note's own acute red-flag screen is NEGATIVE (blood pressure maintained on standing = no orthostatic hypotension, moist mucous membranes = no severe dehydration, regular rhythm/no murmur = no cardiogenic arrhythmia on exam, normal oxygen saturation/clear chest = no PE, heart rate returns to normal immediately on lying flat) and the in-person disposition is routine (GP review in 1 week). The telehealth-correct answer is to RECOGNISE the known benign post-viral chronic dysautonomia pattern, tier T2 (prompt GP + specialist/autonomic-testing referral + fluid/salt + counter-manoeuvre advice + fall precautions), and attach PROMINENT MANDATORY escalation safety-netting. Over-triage to ED/000 as the baseline disposition is the scored defect.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person home visit where the clinician measured active-stand postural vital signs (BP 115/75 supine → 110/75 standing, HR 72 → 128, +56 bpm), auscultated the heart and assessed mucous membranes. Breath-Ezy cannot measure orthostatic vitals, auscultate, or assess membrane hydration — those clinician measurements are sealed in 10/11; the AI Doctor works from the patient's lay report of postural symptoms.

Age inferred as adolescent (~16) from 'Present at Visit: Patient and parent' plus reliance on parent for hydration and safety and body weight 65 kg — flagged; triggers paediatric consideration (no paediatric dose synthesised; beta-blocker/ivabradine remain GP-initiated after workup).
Sex inferred as female from post-viral dysautonomia / POTS epidemiology (strong female predominance) — not stated in the source; flagged.
Red-herring: the cardiac-sounding tachycardia is a red herring for cardiogenic syncope — the discriminator is that it is strictly postural and resolves fully on lying flat.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source note CFE-024.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
